description: Simplify Navbar design and make it ultra-functional. Reduce visual complexity, make the navigation bar cleaner with minimal elements, improve mobile menu UX. The current navbar has excessive cyber-themed elements (UPLINK_ON status, animated ping circles, complex glass morphism). Simplify to a clean, minimal floating nav pill with essential navigation links and a streamlined mobile menu.
steps: Rewrite src/components/Navbar.tsx: Remove the 'UPLINK_ON' status indicator, simplify the logo to just text 'FR' without the ping animation circle. Keep navigation links but make them cleaner (remove // prefix from labels). Make the glass capsule always visible with subtle background. Simplify the mobile hamburger button.

Simplify the mobile menu panel: Remove the tab switcher (navigation/settings), merge it into a single clean panel with navigation links at top, a compact theme toggle (light/dark/system), and language switcher at bottom. Remove the TableOfContents component with its scroll progress bar - just use simple nav links. Keep the SettingsPanel accessible via a gear icon button that opens the full settings.
Update src/components/menu/TableOfContents.tsx: Simplify or remove - navigation links will be directly in the mobile menu panel.

Update nav item labels in all locale files (src/locales/en.json, id.json, zh.json): Change from '// HOME', '// BLUEPRINT' etc to cleaner labels: 'Home', 'About', 'Projects', 'Skills', 'Contact' (and translations).
Ensure the navbar works well in both light and dark themes with proper color transitions.
acceptance_criteria: Navbar is visually simpler - no cyber-themed decorations, no ping animations, no UPLINK_ON status
Desktop nav shows clean text links with active indicator
Mobile menu opens as a slide panel with direct navigation links, theme toggle, and language switcher
All navigation functionality preserved (scroll to section, active section tracking)
Build passes with npm run build
